hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions. This should increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) without compromising SSTR2 affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs show promising ddG but insufficient structural quality and limited selectivity improvement over native SST-14. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions will enhance selectivity and structural quality.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tighten clash tolerance to improve structural quality and reduce false positives in binding.
Target non-pharmacophore positions for mutations to disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions while preserving pharmacophore residues (7-10) and cysteines.
